summary: Problem 9 concerns combinatorial identities for lattice paths (algebraic combinatorics). There is currently no substantive proof attempt on record — the 'best proof' entry is a placeholder (model 'simple', flagged as 100 open issues) rather than a worked argument, and the single tracked issue contains only an area/author stub without a stated theorem or proof draft. The work is effectively at the kickoff stage and needs the problem statement extracted and a proof strategy established before any gap analysis is meaningful.
problems: No actual proof content exists yet: the best-proof record is a placeholder ('simple' model, 'status: 100 open issues'), so there is no argument to evaluate for correctness or completeness.

The lone open issue ('Proof: Problem 9: Combinatorial Identities for Lattice Paths') carries only metadata (area=combinatorics/algebraic combinatorics/lattice paths, author unknown) and a truncated problem statement — the precise identity to be proven is not yet captured in the tracker.
The mathematical blocker cannot be localized because neither the target identity nor a candidate proof method (bijective, generating-function, or reflection-principle) has been recorded.
Inconsistent issue counts across the context (1 issue listed vs. '100 open issues' in the best-proof status) indicate metadata/state desync that should be reconciled before triage.
highlights: The problem domain is well-scoped and tractable: lattice-path identities typically admit standard tools (bijections, the reflection principle, Lindström–Gessel–Viennot, or generating functions), so a clear strategy is achievable once the statement is pinned down.
A single open issue keeps the triage surface small and focused for the initial proof-writing pass.

suggested_todos.title: Extract the full prob-09 problem statement from the LaTeX source and record the exact lattice-path identity to be proven in the issue
Draft an initial proof strategy choosing among bijective, reflection-principle, or generating-function approaches and attach it to the open issue
Replace the placeholder 'simple' best-proof entry with a first real proof skeleton (statement, lemmas, proof outline)
Reconcile the conflicting issue counts (1 vs. 100 open issues) so the tracker reflects true state
Identify and cite the standard reference results (e.g., LGV lemma, Catalan/ballot identities) likely needed for the proof
